385: // SetupBeadsTracking determines beads ID and manages issue lifecycle.
386: // Returns final beads ID (empty if untracked), or error if setup fails.
387: func SetupBeadsTracking(skillName, task, projectName, beadsIssueFlag string, isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator bool, serverURL string, noTrack bool, createBeadsFn func(string, string, string) (string, error)) (string, error) {
388: 	// Determine beads ID - either from flag, create new issue, or skip if --no-track
389: 	// Orchestrators skip beads tracking entirely - they're interactive sessions with Dylan,
390: 	// not autonomous tasks. SESSION_HANDOFF.md is richer than beads comments.
391: 	skipBeadsForOrchestrator := isOrchestrator || isMetaOrchestrator
392: 	beadsID, err := determineBeadsID(projectName, skillName, task, beadsIssueFlag, noTrack || skipBeadsForOrchestrator, createBeadsFn)
393: 	if err != nil {
394: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to determine beads ID: %w", err)
395: 	}
396: 	if skipBeadsForOrchestrator {
397: 		fmt.Println("Skipping beads tracking (orchestrator session)")
398: 	} else if noTrack {
399: 		fmt.Println("Skipping beads tracking (--no-track)")
400: 	}
401: 
402: 	// Check for retry patterns on existing issues - surface to prevent blind respawning
403: 	// Skip for orchestrators since they don't use beads tracking
404: 	if !noTrack && !skipBeadsForOrchestrator && beadsIssueFlag != "" {
405: 		if stats, err := verify.GetFixAttemptStats(beadsID); err == nil && stats.IsRetryPattern() {
406: 			warning := verify.FormatRetryWarning(stats)
407: 			if warning != "" {
408: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "\n%s\n", warning)
409: 			}
410: 		}
411: 	}
412: 
413: 	// Check if issue is already being worked on (prevent duplicate spawns)
414: 	// Skip for orchestrators since they don't use beads tracking
415: 	if !noTrack && !skipBeadsForOrchestrator && beadsIssueFlag != "" {
416: 		if issue, err := verify.GetIssue(beadsID); err == nil {
417: 			if issue.Status == "closed" {
418: 				return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s is already closed", beadsID)
419: 			}
420: 			if issue.Status == "in_progress" {
421: 				// Check if there's a truly active agent for this issue
422: 				// OpenCode persists sessions to disk, so we must verify liveness not just existence
423: 				client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
424: 				sessions, _ := client.ListSessions("")
425: 				for _, s := range sessions {
426: 					if strings.Contains(s.Title, beadsID) {
427: 						// Session exists - but is it actually active (recently updated)?
428: 						// Use 30 minute threshold - if no activity, session is stale
429: 						if client.IsSessionActive(s.ID, 30*time.Minute) {
430: 							return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s is already in_progress with active agent (session %s). Use 'orch send %s' to interact or 'orch abandon %s' to restart", beadsID, s.ID, s.ID, beadsID)
431: 						}
432: 						// Session exists but is stale - log and continue (allow respawn)
433: 						fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Note: found stale session %s for issue %s (no activity in 30m)\n", s.ID[:12], beadsID)
434: 					}
435: 				}
436: 				// No active session - check if Phase: Complete was reported
437: 				// If so, orchestrator needs to run 'orch complete' before respawning
438: 				if complete, err := verify.IsPhaseComplete(beadsID); err == nil && complete {
439: 					return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s has Phase: Complete but is not closed. Run 'orch complete %s' first", beadsID, beadsID)
440: 				}
441: 				// In progress but no active agent and not Phase: Complete - warn but allow respawn
442: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: issue %s is in_progress but no active agent found. Respawning.\n", beadsID)
443: 			}
444: 		}
445: 	}
446: 
447: 	// Update beads issue status to in_progress (only if tracking a real issue)
448: 	// Skip for orchestrators since they don't use beads tracking
449: 	if !noTrack && !skipBeadsForOrchestrator && beadsIssueFlag != "" {
450: 		if err := verify.UpdateIssueStatus(beadsID, "in_progress"); err != nil {
451: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update beads issue status: %v\n", err)
452: 			// Continue anyway
453: 		}
454: 	}
455: 
456: 	return beadsID, nil
457: }

459: // ResolveAndValidateModel resolves model aliases and validates model choice.
460: // Returns error if flash model is requested (unsupported).
461: func ResolveAndValidateModel(modelFlag string) (model.ModelSpec, error) {
462: 	// Resolve model - convert aliases to full format
463: 	resolvedModel := model.Resolve(modelFlag)
464: 
465: 	// Validate flash model - TPM rate limits make it unusable
466: 	if resolvedModel.Provider == "google" && strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(resolvedModel.ModelID), "flash") {
467: 		return resolvedModel, fmt.Errorf(`
468: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
469: │  🚫 Flash model not supported                                                │
470: ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
471: │  Gemini Flash has TPM (tokens per minute) rate limits that make it           │
472: │  unsuitable for agent work. Use sonnet (default) or opus instead.            │
473: │                                                                             │
474: │  Available options:                                                         │
475: │    • --model sonnet  (default, pay-per-token via OpenCode)                  │
476: │    • --model opus    (Max subscription via claude CLI)                      │
477: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
478: `)
479: 	}
480: 
481: 	return resolvedModel, nil
482: }

484: // GatherSpawnContext gathers KB context and performs gap analysis.
485: // Returns context string, gap analysis, model injection info, or error.
486: func GatherSpawnContext(skillContent, task, beadsID, projectDir string, skipArtifactCheck, gateOnGap, skipGapGate bool, gapThreshold int) (
487: 	kbContext string,
488: 	gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis,
489: 	hasInjectedModels bool,
490: 	primaryModelPath string,
491: 	err error) {
492: 
493: 	if skipArtifactCheck {
494: 		fmt.Println("Skipping context check (--skip-artifact-check)")
495: 		return "", nil, false, "", nil
496: 	}
497: 
498: 	// Parse skill requirements to determine what context to gather
499: 	requires := spawn.ParseSkillRequires(skillContent)
500: 
501: 	if requires != nil && requires.HasRequirements() {
502: 		// Skill-driven context gathering
503: 		fmt.Printf("Gathering context (skill requires: %s)\n", requires.String())
504: 		kbContext = spawn.GatherRequiredContext(requires, task, beadsID, projectDir)
505: 		// For skill-driven context, create a basic gap analysis from the results
506: 		// This is a placeholder - skills may provide their own gap info
507: 		gapAnalysis = spawn.AnalyzeGaps(nil, task)
508: 	} else {
509: 		// Fall back to default kb context check with full gap analysis
510: 		gapResult := runPreSpawnKBCheckFull(task)
511: 		kbContext = gapResult.Context
512: 		gapAnalysis = gapResult.GapAnalysis
513: 
514: 		// Extract model injection info for probe vs investigation routing
515: 		if gapResult.FormatResult != nil {
516: 			hasInjectedModels = gapResult.FormatResult.HasInjectedModels
517: 			if hasInjectedModels {
518: 				// Extract primary model path from KB context result
519: 				primaryModelPath = extractPrimaryModelPath(gapResult.FormatResult)
520: 			}
521: 		}
522: 	}
523: 
524: 	// Check gap gating - may block spawn if context quality is too low
525: 	if err := checkGapGating(gapAnalysis, gateOnGap, skipGapGate, gapThreshold); err != nil {
526: 		return "", nil, false, "", err
527: 	}
528: 
529: 	// Record gap for learning loop (if gaps detected)
530: 	if gapAnalysis != nil && gapAnalysis.HasGaps {
531: 		recordGapForLearning(gapAnalysis, skillContent, task)
532: 	}
533: 
534: 	// Log if skip-gap-gate was used (documents conscious bypass)
535: 	if skipGapGate && gapAnalysis != nil && gapAnalysis.ShouldBlockSpawn(gapThreshold) {
536: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "⚠️  Bypassing gap gate (--skip-gap-gate): context quality %d\n", gapAnalysis.ContextQuality)
537: 		// Log the bypass for pattern detection
538: 		logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
539: 		event := events.Event{
540: 			Type:      "gap.gate.bypassed",
541: 			Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
542: 			Data: map[string]interface{}{
543: 				"task":            task,
544: 				"context_quality": gapAnalysis.ContextQuality,
545: 				"beads_id":        beadsID,
546: 				"skill":           skillContent,
547: 			},
548: 		}
549: 		if err := logger.Log(event); err != nil {
550: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log gap bypass: %v\n", err)
551: 		}
552: 	}
553: 
554: 	return kbContext, gapAnalysis, hasInjectedModels, primaryModelPath, nil
555: }

590: // DetermineSpawnBackend determines spawn backend with auto-selection.
591: // Priority: --backend flag > --opus flag > infrastructure detection > model-based > config > default.
592: func DetermineSpawnBackend(resolvedModel model.ModelSpec, task, beadsID, projectDir, backendFlag, spawnModel string, opusFlag bool) (string, error) {
593: 	// Load project config (used for backend default)
594: 	projCfg, _ := config.Load(projectDir)
595: 
596: 	// Default to claude (Max subscription covers Claude CLI usage)
597: 	backend := "claude"
598: 
599: 	if backendFlag != "" {
600: 		// Explicit --backend flag: highest priority
601: 		backend = backendFlag
602: 		// Validate backend value
603: 		if backend != "claude" && backend != "opencode" {
604: 			return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid --backend value: %s (must be 'claude' or 'opencode')", backend)
605: 		}
606: 	} else if opusFlag {
607: 		// Explicit --opus flag: use claude CLI
608: 		backend = "claude"
609: 	} else if isInfrastructureWork(task, beadsID) {
610: 		// Infrastructure work detection: auto-apply escape hatch
611: 		// Agents working on OpenCode/orch infrastructure need claude backend + tmux
612: 		// to survive server restarts (prevent agents from killing themselves)
613: 		backend = "claude"
614: 		fmt.Println("🔧 Infrastructure work detected - auto-applying escape hatch (--backend claude --tmux)")
615: 		fmt.Println("   This ensures the agent survives OpenCode server restarts.")
616: 
617: 		// Log the infrastructure work detection for pattern analysis
618: 		logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
619: 		event := events.Event{
620: 			Type:      "spawn.infrastructure_detected",
621: 			Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
622: 			Data: map[string]interface{}{
623: 				"task":     task,
624: 				"beads_id": beadsID,
625: 				"skill":    "", // Will be filled by caller
626: 			},
627: 		}
628: 		if err := logger.Log(event); err != nil {
629: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log infrastructure detection: %v\n", err)
630: 		}
631: 	} else if spawnModel != "" {
632: 		// Auto-select backend based on model
633: 		modelLower := strings.ToLower(spawnModel)
634: 		if modelLower == "opus" || strings.Contains(modelLower, "opus") {
635: 			// Opus model: use claude CLI (Max subscription)
636: 			backend = "claude"
637: 			fmt.Println("Auto-selected claude backend for opus model")
638: 		} else if modelLower == "sonnet" || strings.Contains(modelLower, "sonnet") {
639: 			// Sonnet model: use opencode (pay-per-token API)
640: 			backend = "opencode"
641: 		}
642: 		// Other models keep the default backend (claude)
643: 	} else if projCfg != nil && projCfg.SpawnMode == "claude" {
644: 		// Config default: respect project spawn_mode setting
645: 		backend = "claude"
646: 	}
647: 
648: 	// Validate mode+model combination
649: 	if err := validateModeModelCombo(backend, resolvedModel); err != nil {
650: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "⚠️  %v\n", err)
651: 	}
652: 
653: 	return backend, nil
654: }

710: // ValidateAndWriteContext validates context size, writes SPAWN_CONTEXT.md, and generates prompt.
711: // Returns minimal prompt, or error if validation fails.
712: func ValidateAndWriteContext(cfg *spawn.Config, force bool) (minimalPrompt string, err error) {
713: 	// Pre-spawn token estimation and validation
714: 	if err := spawn.ValidateContextSize(cfg); err != nil {
715: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("pre-spawn validation failed: %w", err)
716: 	}
717: 
718: 	// Warn about large contexts (but don't block)
719: 	if shouldWarn, warning := spawn.ShouldWarnAboutSize(cfg); shouldWarn {
720: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "%s", warning)
721: 	}
722: 
723: 	// Check for existing workspace before writing context
724: 	// This prevents accidentally overwriting SESSION_HANDOFF.md from completed sessions
725: 	// Note: With unique suffixes in workspace names (since Jan 2026), collisions are rare
726: 	// but this provides an extra safety net and meaningful error messages
727: 	if err := checkWorkspaceExists(cfg.WorkspacePath(), force); err != nil {
728: 		return "", err
729: 	}
730: 
731: 	// Write SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
732: 	if err := spawn.WriteContext(cfg); err != nil {
733: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to write spawn context: %w", err)
734: 	}
735: 
736: 	// Record spawn in session (if session is active)
737: 	if sessionStore, err := session.New(""); err == nil {
738: 		if err := sessionStore.RecordSpawn(cfg.BeadsID, cfg.SkillName, cfg.Task, cfg.ProjectDir); err != nil {
739: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to record spawn in session: %v\n", err)
740: 		}
741: 	}
742: 
743: 	// Generate minimal prompt
744: 	minimalPrompt = spawn.MinimalPrompt(cfg)
745: 	return minimalPrompt, nil
746: }

748: // DispatchSpawn routes to the appropriate spawn mode function.
749: // Handles inline, headless, claude, and tmux modes.
750: func DispatchSpawn(input *SpawnInput, cfg *spawn.Config, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task, serverURL string) error {
751: 	// Spawn mode: inline (blocking TUI), tmux (opt-in for workers, default for orchestrators), claude (tmux), or headless (default for workers)
752: 	if input.Inline {
753: 		// Inline mode (blocking) - run in current terminal with TUI
754: 		return runSpawnInline(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task)
755: 	}
756: 
757: 	// Explicit --headless flag overrides all other mode decisions
758: 	if input.Headless {
759: 		return runSpawnHeadless(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task)
760: 	}
761: 
762: 	// Claude mode: Use tmux + claude CLI
763: 	if cfg.SpawnMode == "claude" {
764: 		return runSpawnClaude(serverURL, cfg, beadsID, skillName, task, input.Attach)
765: 	}
766: 
767: 	// Orchestrator-type skills default to tmux mode (visible interaction)
768: 	// Workers default to headless mode (automation-friendly)
769: 	useTmux := input.Tmux || input.Attach || cfg.IsOrchestrator
770: 	if useTmux {
771: 		// Tmux mode - visible, interruptible
772: 		// Default for orchestrator skills, opt-in for workers
773: 		return runSpawnTmux(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task, input.Attach)
774: 	}
775: 
776: 	// Default for workers: Headless mode - spawn via HTTP API (automation-friendly, no TUI overhead)
777: 	return runSpawnHeadless(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task)
778: }

797: // registerOrchestratorSession registers an orchestrator session in the session registry.
798: // This is called after successful spawn for orchestrator-type skills.
799: // Workers do not use the session registry - they use beads for lifecycle tracking.
800: func registerOrchestratorSession(cfg *spawn.Config, sessionID, task string) {
801: 	if !cfg.IsOrchestrator && !cfg.IsMetaOrchestrator {
802: 		return // Only register orchestrator sessions
803: 	}
804: 
805: 	registry := session.NewRegistry("")
806: 	orchSession := session.OrchestratorSession{
807: 		WorkspaceName: cfg.WorkspaceName,
808: 		SessionID:     sessionID,
809: 		ProjectDir:    cfg.ProjectDir,
810: 		SpawnTime:     time.Now(),
811: 		Goal:          task,
812: 		Status:        "active",
813: 	}
814: 	if err := registry.Register(orchSession); err != nil {
815: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to register orchestrator session: %v\n", err)
816: 	}
817: }

819: // addGapAnalysisToEventData adds gap analysis information to an event data map.
820: // This enables tracking of context gaps for pattern analysis and dashboard surfacing.
821: func addGapAnalysisToEventData(eventData map[string]interface{}, gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis) {
822: 	if gapAnalysis == nil {
823: 		return
824: 	}
825: 
826: 	eventData["gap_has_gaps"] = gapAnalysis.HasGaps
827: 	eventData["gap_context_quality"] = gapAnalysis.ContextQuality
828: 
829: 	if gapAnalysis.HasGaps {
830: 		eventData["gap_should_warn"] = gapAnalysis.ShouldWarnAboutGaps()
831: 		eventData["gap_match_total"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.TotalMatches
832: 		eventData["gap_match_constraints"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.ConstraintCount
833: 		eventData["gap_match_decisions"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.DecisionCount
834: 		eventData["gap_match_investigations"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.InvestigationCount
835: 
836: 		// Capture gap types for pattern analysis
837: 		var gapTypes []string
838: 		for _, gap := range gapAnalysis.Gaps {
839: 			gapTypes = append(gapTypes, string(gap.Type))
840: 		}
841: 		if len(gapTypes) > 0 {
842: 			eventData["gap_types"] = gapTypes
843: 		}
844: 	}
845: }
846: 
847: // addUsageInfoToEventData adds usage information to an event data map.
848: // This enables tracking of rate limit patterns and account utilization at spawn time.
849: func addUsageInfoToEventData(eventData map[string]interface{}, usageInfo *spawn.UsageInfo) {
850: 	if usageInfo == nil {
851: 		return
852: 	}
853: 
854: 	eventData["usage_5h_used"] = usageInfo.FiveHourUsed
855: 	eventData["usage_weekly_used"] = usageInfo.SevenDayUsed
856: 	if usageInfo.AccountEmail != "" {
857: 		eventData["usage_account"] = usageInfo.AccountEmail
858: 	}
859: 	if usageInfo.AutoSwitched {
860: 		eventData["usage_auto_switched"] = true
861: 		eventData["usage_switch_reason"] = usageInfo.SwitchReason
862: 	}
863: }

865: // formatContextQualitySummary formats context quality for spawn summary output.
866: // Returns a formatted string with visual indicators for gap severity.
867: // This is the "prominent" surfacing that makes gaps hard to ignore.
868: func formatContextQualitySummary(gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis) string {
869: 	if gapAnalysis == nil {
870: 		return "not checked"
871: 	}
872: 
873: 	quality := gapAnalysis.ContextQuality
874: 
875: 	// Determine visual indicator and label based on quality level
876: 	var indicator, label string
877: 	switch {
878: 	case quality == 0:
879: 		indicator = "🚨"
880: 		label = "CRITICAL - No context"
881: 	case quality < 20:
882: 		indicator = "⚠️"
883: 		label = "poor"
884: 	case quality < 40:
885: 		indicator = "⚠️"
886: 		label = "limited"
887: 	case quality < 60:
888: 		indicator = "📊"
889: 		label = "moderate"
890: 	case quality < 80:
891: 		indicator = "✓"
892: 		label = "good"
893: 	default:
894: 		indicator = "✓"
895: 		label = "excellent"
896: 	}
897: 
898: 	// Format the summary line
899: 	summary := fmt.Sprintf("%s %d/100 (%s)", indicator, quality, label)
900: 
901: 	// Add match breakdown for transparency
902: 	if gapAnalysis.MatchStats.TotalMatches > 0 {
903: 		summary += fmt.Sprintf(" - %d matches", gapAnalysis.MatchStats.TotalMatches)
904: 		if gapAnalysis.MatchStats.ConstraintCount > 0 {
905: 			summary += fmt.Sprintf(" (%d constraints)", gapAnalysis.MatchStats.ConstraintCount)
906: 		}
907: 	}
908: 
909: 	return summary
910: }
911: 
912: // printSpawnSummaryWithGapWarning prints the spawn summary with prominent gap warnings.
913: // This ensures gaps are visible in the final output, not just during context gathering.
914: func printSpawnSummaryWithGapWarning(gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis) {
915: 	if gapAnalysis == nil || !gapAnalysis.ShouldWarnAboutGaps() {
916: 		return
917: 	}
918: 
919: 	// Print a prominent warning box for critical gaps
920: 	if gapAnalysis.HasCriticalGaps() || gapAnalysis.ContextQuality < 20 {
921: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "\n")
922: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n")
923: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "│  ⚠️  GAP WARNING: Agent spawned with limited context         │\n")
924: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n")
925: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "│  Agent may compensate by guessing patterns/conventions.    │\n")
926: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "│  Consider: kn decide / kn constrain / kb create            │\n")
927: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n")
928: 	}
929: }

931: // runSpawnInline spawns the agent inline (blocking) using the HTTP API.
932: // Uses CreateSession + SendMessageInDirectory to properly pass x-opencode-directory
933: // header, ensuring the session is created in the correct project directory.
934: // Blocks until the session completes by watching SSE events.

1387: // CreateBeadsIssue creates a new beads issue for tracking the agent.
1388: // It uses the beads RPC client when available, falling back to the bd CLI.
1389: func CreateBeadsIssue(projectName, skillName, task string) (string, error) {
1390: 	// Build issue title
1391: 	title := fmt.Sprintf("[%s] %s: %s", projectName, skillName, truncate(task, 50))
1392: 
1393: 	// Try RPC client first
1394: 	socketPath, err := beads.FindSocketPath("")
1395: 	if err == nil {
1396: 		client := beads.NewClient(socketPath)
1397: 		if err := client.Connect(); err == nil {
1398: 			defer client.Close()
1399: 
1400: 			issue, err := client.Create(&beads.CreateArgs{
1401: 				Title:     title,
1402: 				IssueType: "task",
1403: 				Priority:  2, // Default P2
1404: 			})
1405: 			if err == nil {
1406: 				return issue.ID, nil
1407: 			}
1408: 			// Fall through to CLI fallback on RPC error
1409: 		}
1410: 	}
1411: 
1412: 	// Fallback to CLI
1413: 	issue, err := beads.FallbackCreate(title, "", "task", 2, nil)
1414: 	if err != nil {
1415: 		return "", err
1416: 	}
1417: 
1418: 	return issue.ID, nil
1419: }

1436: func checkWorkspaceExists(workspacePath string, force bool) error {

1447: 	}

1449: 	for _, file := range criticalFiles {

1472: // runPreSpawnKBCheck runs kb context check before spawning an agent.

1474: // Also performs gap analysis and displays warnings for sparse or missing context.

1480: // runPreSpawnKBCheckFull runs kb context check with full gap analysis results.
1481: // This allows callers to access gap analysis for gating decisions.
1482: func runPreSpawnKBCheckFull(task string) *GapCheckResult {
1483: 	gcr := &GapCheckResult{}
1484: 
1485: 	// Extract keywords from task description
1486: 	// Try with 3 keywords first (more specific), fall back to 1 keyword (more broad)
1487: 	keywords := spawn.ExtractKeywords(task, 3)
1488: 	if keywords == "" {
1489: 		// Perform gap analysis even when no keywords extracted
1490: 		gcr.GapAnalysis = spawn.AnalyzeGaps(nil, task)
1491: 		if gcr.GapAnalysis.ShouldWarnAboutGaps() {
1492: 			// Use prominent warning format for better visibility
1493: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "%s", gcr.GapAnalysis.FormatProminentWarning())
1494: 		}
1495: 		return gcr
1496: 	}
1497: 
1498: 	fmt.Printf("Checking kb context for: %q\n", keywords)
1499: 
1500: 	// Run kb context check
1501: 	result, err := spawn.RunKBContextCheck(keywords)
1502: 	if err != nil {
1503: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: kb context check failed: %v\n", err)
1504: 		return gcr
1505: 	}
1506: 
1507: 	// If no matches with multiple keywords, try with just the first keyword
1508: 	if result == nil || !result.HasMatches {
1509: 		firstKeyword := spawn.ExtractKeywords(task, 1)
1510: 		if firstKeyword != "" && firstKeyword != keywords {
1511: 			fmt.Printf("Trying broader search for: %q\n", firstKeyword)
1512: 			result, err = spawn.RunKBContextCheck(firstKeyword)
1513: 			if err != nil {
1514: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: kb context check failed: %v\n", err)
1515: 				return gcr
1516: 			}
1517: 		}
1518: 	}
1519: 
1520: 	// Perform gap analysis to detect context gaps
1521: 	gcr.GapAnalysis = spawn.AnalyzeGaps(result, keywords)
1522: 	if gcr.GapAnalysis.ShouldWarnAboutGaps() {
1523: 		// Use prominent warning format for better visibility
1524: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "%s", gcr.GapAnalysis.FormatProminentWarning())
1525: 	}
1526: 
1527: 	if result == nil || !result.HasMatches {
1528: 		fmt.Println("No prior knowledge found.")
1529: 		return gcr
1530: 	}
1531: 
1532: 	// Always include kb context in spawn - the orchestrator has already decided to spawn
1533: 	// No interactive prompt needed; context is automatically included
1534: 	fmt.Printf("Found %d relevant context entries - including in spawn context.\n", len(result.Matches))
1535: 
1536: 	// Format context with limit and capture full result (includes HasInjectedModels)
1537: 	formatResult := spawn.FormatContextForSpawnWithLimit(result, spawn.MaxKBContextChars)
1538: 	gcr.FormatResult = formatResult
1539: 
1540: 	// Include gap summary in spawn context if there are significant gaps
1541: 	contextContent := formatResult.Content
1542: 	if gapSummary := gcr.GapAnalysis.FormatGapSummary(); gapSummary != "" {
1543: 		contextContent = gapSummary + "\n\n" + contextContent
1544: 	}
1545: 
1546: 	gcr.Context = contextContent
1547: 	return gcr
1548: }

1550: // checkGapGating checks if spawn should be blocked due to context gaps.
1551: // Returns an error if spawn should be blocked, nil otherwise.
1552: func checkGapGating(gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis, gateEnabled, skipGate bool, threshold int) error {
1553: 	// Skip gating if not enabled or explicitly bypassed
1554: 	if !gateEnabled || skipGate {
1555: 		return nil
1556: 	}
1557: 
1558: 	// No gap analysis means no gating
1559: 	if gapAnalysis == nil {
1560: 		return nil
1561: 	}
1562: 
1563: 	// Check if quality is below threshold
1564: 	if threshold <= 0 {
1565: 		threshold = spawn.DefaultGateThreshold
1566: 	}
1567: 
1568: 	if gapAnalysis.ShouldBlockSpawn(threshold) {
1569: 		// Display the block message
1570: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "%s", gapAnalysis.FormatGateBlockMessage())
1571: 		return fmt.Errorf("spawn blocked: context quality %d is below threshold %d", gapAnalysis.ContextQuality, threshold)
1572: 	}
1573: 
1574: 	return nil
1575: }

1577: // recordGapForLearning records a gap event for the learning loop.
1578: // This builds up a history of gaps that can be used to suggest improvements.
1579: func recordGapForLearning(gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis, skill, task string) {
1580: 	// Load existing tracker
1581: 	tracker, err := spawn.LoadTracker()
1582: 	if err != nil {
1583: 		// Don't fail spawn for learning loop errors
1584: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load gap tracker: %v\n", err)
1585: 		return
1586: 	}
1587: 
1588: 	// Record the gap
1589: 	tracker.RecordGap(gapAnalysis, skill, task)
1590: 
1591: 	// Check for recurring patterns and display suggestions
1592: 	suggestions := tracker.FindRecurringGaps()
1593: 	if len(suggestions) > 0 {
1594: 		// Only show suggestions if there are high-priority ones
1595: 		hasHighPriority := false
1596: 		for _, s := range suggestions {
1597: 			if s.Priority == "high" && s.Count >= spawn.RecurrenceThreshold {
1598: 				hasHighPriority = true
1599: 				break
1600: 			}
1601: 		}
1602: 		if hasHighPriority {
1603: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "%s", spawn.FormatSuggestions(suggestions))
1604: 		}
1605: 	}
1606: 
1607: 	// Save tracker
1608: 	if err := tracker.Save(); err != nil {
1609: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to save gap tracker: %v\n", err)
1610: 	}
1611: }

1684: // Returns mockup path, prompt path, and design notes from SYNTHESIS.md.

1686: func readDesignArtifacts(projectDir, designWorkspace string) (mockupPath, promptPath, designNotes string) {
1687: 	workspacePath := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".orch", "workspace", designWorkspace)
1688:

1701: 				mockupPath = filepath.Join(screenshotsPath, entry.Name())
1702: 				// Check for corresponding .prompt.md file

1704: 				promptPath = filepath.Join(screenshotsPath, promptName)
1705: 				if _, err := os.Stat(promptPath); err != nil {

1711: 	}
1712: 
1713: 	// Read design notes from SYNTHESIS.md
1714: 	synthesisPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SYNTHESIS.md")

1724: // extractDesignNotes extracts relevant sections from SYNTHESIS.md for design handoff.
1725: // Returns TLDR and Knowledge sections which contain key design insights.
1726: func extractDesignNotes(content string) string {
1727: 	var notes strings.Builder
1728: 
1729: 	// Extract TLDR section
1730: 	if tldr := extractSection(content, "## TLDR"); tldr != "" {
1731: 		notes.WriteString("**Design TLDR:**\n")
1732: 		notes.WriteString(tldr)
1733: 		notes.WriteString("\n\n")
1734: 	}
1735: 
1736: 	// Extract Knowledge section
1737: 	if knowledge := extractSection(content, "## Knowledge"); knowledge != "" {
1738: 		notes.WriteString("**Design Knowledge:**\n")
1739: 		notes.WriteString(knowledge)
1740: 	}
1741: 
1742: 	return notes.String()
1743: }
1744: 
1745: // extractSection extracts content between a section header and the next ## header.
1746: // Returns empty string if section not found.
1747: func extractSection(content, sectionHeader string) string {
1748: 	lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
1749: 	var sectionLines []string
1750: 	inSection := false
1751: 
1752: 	for _, line := range lines {
1753: 		if strings.HasPrefix(line, sectionHeader) {
1754: 			inSection = true
1755: 			continue
1756: 		}
1757: 		if inSection && strings.HasPrefix(line, "##") {
1758: 			break // Reached next section
1759: 		}
1760: 		if inSection {
1761: 			sectionLines = append(sectionLines, line)
1762: 		}
1763: 	}
1764: 
1765: 	if len(sectionLines) == 0 {
1766: 		return ""
1767: 	}
1768: 
1769: 	return strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(sectionLines, "\n"))
1770: }
1771: 
1772: // truncate truncates a string to a maximum length, adding "..." if truncated.
1773: func truncate(s string, maxLen int) string {
1774: 	if len(s) <= maxLen {
1775: 		return s
1776: 	}
1777: 	return s[:maxLen-3] + "..."
1778: }

1782: func resolveShortBeadsID(id string) (string, error) {
1783: 	// If already in full format, return as-is
1784: 	if strings.Contains(id, "-") {
1785: 		return id, nil
1786: 	}

1788: 	// Otherwise, we need to get the project name from current directory

1790: 	if err != nil {
1791: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current directory: %w", err)
1792: 	}
1793: 	projectName := filepath.Base(projectDir)
1794: 
1795: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", projectName, id), nil
1796: }

1236: // runSpawnInline spawns the agent inline (blocking) using the HTTP API.
1237: // Uses CreateSession + SendMessageInDirectory to properly pass x-opencode-directory
1238: // header, ensuring the session is created in the correct project directory.
1239: // Blocks until the session completes by watching SSE events.
1240: func runSpawnInline(serverURL string, cfg *spawn.Config, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task string) error {

1242: 	sessionTitle := formatSessionTitle(cfg.WorkspaceName, beadsID)
1243:

1245: 	// CreateSession passes x-opencode-directory header so the server uses the target project dir
1246: 	metadata := map[string]string{
1247: 		"beads_id":       cfg.BeadsID,
1248: 		"workspace_path": cfg.WorkspacePath(),
1249: 		"tier":           cfg.Tier,
1250: 		"spawn_mode":     "inline",
1251: 	}
1252: 
1253: 	// Calculate TTL based on session type
1254: 	// Worker sessions: 4 hours (14400 seconds)
1255: 	// Orchestrator sessions: 0 (no expiration)
1256: 	timeTTL := 4 * 60 * 60 // 4 hours in seconds
1257: 	if cfg.IsOrchestrator {
1258: 		timeTTL = 0 // Orchestrator sessions persist until manually cleaned

1261: 	session, err := client.CreateSession(sessionTitle, cfg.ProjectDir, cfg.Model, metadata, timeTTL)

1332: // runSpawnTmux spawns the agent in a tmux window (interactive, returns immediately).
1333: // Creates a tmux window in workers-{project} session (or orchestrator session for orchestrator skills).
1334: func runSpawnTmux(serverURL string, cfg *spawn.Config, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task string, attach bool) error {
1335: 	var sessionName string
1336: 	var err error
1337: 
1338: 	// Meta-orchestrators and orchestrators go into 'orchestrator' tmux session
1339: 	// Workers go into 'workers-{project}' session
1340: 	if cfg.IsMetaOrchestrator || cfg.IsOrchestrator {
1341: 		sessionName, err = tmux.EnsureOrchestratorSession()
1342: 	} else {
1343: 		sessionName, err = tmux.EnsureWorkersSession(cfg.Project, cfg.ProjectDir)
1344: 	}
1345: 	if err != nil {
1346: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to ensure tmux session: %w", err)
1347: 	}
1348: 
1349: 	// Build window name with emoji and beads ID
1350: 	windowName := tmux.BuildWindowName(cfg.WorkspaceName, cfg.SkillName, beadsID)
1351: 
1352: 	// Create new tmux window
1353: 	windowTarget, windowID, err := tmux.CreateWindow(sessionName, windowName, cfg.ProjectDir)
1354: 	if err != nil {
1355: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create tmux window: %w", err)
1356: 	}
1357: 
1358: 	// Build opencode command using tmux package
1359: 	opencodeCmd := tmux.BuildOpencodeAttachCommand(&tmux.OpencodeAttachConfig{
1360: 		ServerURL:  serverURL,
1361: 		ProjectDir: cfg.ProjectDir,
1362: 		Model:      cfg.Model,
1363: 	})
1364: 
1365: 	// Send command and execute
1366: 	if err := tmux.SendKeys(windowTarget, opencodeCmd); err != nil {
1367: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to send opencode command: %w", err)
1368: 	}
1369: 	if err := tmux.SendEnter(windowTarget); err != nil {
1370: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to execute command: %w", err)
1371: 	}
1372: 
1373: 	// Wait for OpenCode TUI to be ready
1374: 	waitCfg := tmux.DefaultWaitConfig()
1375: 	if err := tmux.WaitForOpenCodeReady(windowTarget, waitCfg); err != nil {
1376: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to start opencode: %w", err)
1377: 	}
1378: 
1379: 	// Capture session ID from API with retry (OpenCode may not have registered yet)
1380: 	// Uses 3 attempts with 500ms initial delay, doubling each time (500ms, 1s, 2s)
1381: 	// Matches by directory + creation time (within 30s), not by title
1382: 	client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
1383: 	sessionID, _ := client.FindRecentSessionWithRetry(cfg.ProjectDir, 3, 500*time.Millisecond)
1384: 	// Note: We silently ignore errors here since window_id is sufficient for tmux monitoring
1385: 
1386: 	// Send prompt
1387: 	sendCfg := tmux.DefaultSendPromptConfig()
1388: 	time.Sleep(sendCfg.PostReadyDelay)
1389: 	if err := tmux.SendKeysLiteral(windowTarget, minimalPrompt); err != nil {
1390: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to send prompt: %w", err)
1391: 	}
1392: 	if err := tmux.SendEnter(windowTarget); err != nil {
1393: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to send enter: %w", err)
1394: 	}
1395: 
1396: 	// Write session ID to workspace file for later lookups
1397: 	if sessionID != "" {
1398: 		if err := spawn.WriteSessionID(cfg.WorkspacePath(), sessionID); err != nil {
1399: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to write session ID: %v\n", err)
1400: 		}
1401: 	}
1402: 
1403: 	// Register orchestrator session in registry (workers use beads instead)
1404: 	registerOrchestratorSession(cfg, sessionID, task)
1405: 
1406: 	// Log the session creation
1407: 	logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
1408: 	eventData := map[string]interface{}{
1409: 		"skill":               skillName,
1410: 		"task":                task,
1411: 		"workspace":           cfg.WorkspaceName,
1412: 		"beads_id":            beadsID,

1563: func createBeadsIssue(projectName, skillName, task string) (string, error) {
1564: 	// Build issue title
1565: 	title := fmt.Sprintf("[%s] %s: %s", projectName, skillName, truncate(task, 50))
1566: 
1567: 	// Try RPC client first
1568: 	socketPath, err := beads.FindSocketPath("")
1569: 	if err == nil {

1571: 		if err := client.Connect(); err == nil {

1573: 
1574: 			issue, err := client.Create(&beads.CreateArgs{
1575: 				Title:     title,
1576: 				IssueType: "task",
1577: 				Priority:  2, // Default P2
1578: 			})
1579: 			if err == nil {
1580: 				return issue.ID, nil
1581: 			}
1582: 			// Fall through to CLI fallback on RPC error
1583: 		}
1584: 	}
1585: 
1586: 	// Fallback to CLI
1587: 	issue, err := beads.FallbackCreate(title, "", "task", 2, nil)

<content>1: # Code Extraction Patterns
2: 
3: **Purpose:** Single authoritative reference for extracting code into separate files during refactoring. Read this before starting extraction work.
4: 
5: **Last verified:** 2026-01-08
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## Overview
10: 
11: This guide synthesizes learnings from 13 extraction investigations covering Go packages (serve.go, main.go), Svelte components (+page.svelte, agent-detail panel tabs), and TypeScript services (SSE connection management). It provides proven patterns and gotchas for safely splitting large files.
12: 
13: ---
14: 
15: ## Architecture
16: 
17: ```
18: Before Extraction:           After Extraction:
19: ┌─────────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────────┐
20: │  monolithic.go      │      │  main.go (setup)    │
21: │  - shared utilities │      ├─────────────────────┤
22: │  - domain A code    │  →   │  shared.go          │
23: │  - domain B code    │      ├─────────────────────┤
24: │  - domain C code    │      │  domain_a_cmd.go    │
25: │  (~3000+ lines)     │      │  domain_b_cmd.go    │
26: └─────────────────────┘      │  domain_c_cmd.go    │
27:                              │  (~300-800 lines each)│
28:                              └─────────────────────┘
29: 
30: Extraction Order:
31: 1. Shared utilities FIRST (breaks cross-dependencies)
32: 2. Domain files in parallel (independent units)
33: ```
34: 
35: ---
36: 
37: ## How It Works
38: 
39: ### Phase 1: Extract Shared Utilities First
40: 
41: **What:** Move cross-command/cross-handler functions to a dedicated shared.go file.
42: 
43: **Key insight:** Shared utilities MUST be extracted first. If you extract domain files first, you'll duplicate shared code or create complex import chains.
44: 
45: | Aspect | Details |
46: |--------|---------|
47: | Target file | `shared.go` for Go, `lib/utils/` for Svelte |
48: | Content | Functions used by 2+ domains (e.g., truncate, findWorkspaceByID) |
49: | Line reduction | Usually 200-300 lines from source |
50: 
51: ### Phase 2: Extract Domain-Specific Code
52: 
53: **What:** Move command handlers and their helpers to dedicated files.
54: 
55: **Key insight:** Extract the ENTIRE domain unit together: command definition + flags + init() + run function + types + helpers. Partial extraction creates confusion.
56: 
57: | Naming Convention | Example |
58: |-------------------|---------|
59: | Go commands | `status_cmd.go`, `clean_cmd.go`, `send_cmd.go` |
60: | Go handlers | `serve_agents.go`, `serve_beads.go`, `serve_system.go` |
61: | Svelte components | `lib/components/stats-bar/stats-bar.svelte` |
62: 
63: ### Phase 3: Extract Sub-Domains (If Needed)
64: 
65: **What:** If a domain file grows too large (>800 lines), split further by responsibility.
66: 
67: **Key insight:** Use descriptive suffixes: `serve_agents_cache.go` (cache infrastructure), `serve_agents_events.go` (SSE event handlers).
68: 
69: ### Phase 4: Extract Feature Tabs (Svelte)
70: 
71: **What:** Extract tab/section content from large panel components into dedicated sub-components within the same directory.
72: 
73: **Key insight:** Unlike new component extraction (which creates new directories with barrel exports), feature tab extraction adds files to an existing component directory. Props-based design with `$props()` rune keeps interface clean.
74: 
75: | Aspect | Details |
76: |--------|---------|
77: | Pattern | Large panel → multiple tab components |
78: | Example | `agent-detail-panel.svelte` → `ActivityTab.svelte`, `SynthesisTab.svelte` |
79: | Interface | Single `agent: Agent` prop, self-contained state with `$state()` |
80: | Exports | Add to existing `index.ts` barrel export |
81: | Line reduction | ~200-230 lines per tab |
82: 
83: **When to use:** Panel component exceeds 400 lines with multiple logical sections (tabs, accordions, distinct UI regions).
84: 
85: ### Phase 5: Extract Shared Services (TypeScript)
86: 
87: **What:** Extract duplicate infrastructure patterns (SSE connections, API clients, state management) into shared services.
88: 
89: **Key insight:** Look for the same connection/lifecycle patterns repeated across multiple stores. Extract the infrastructure, keep domain logic in stores.
90: 
91: | Aspect | Details |
92: |--------|---------|
93: | Pattern | Duplicate infrastructure → shared service factory |
94: | Example | SSE connection logic in `agents.ts` + `agentlog.ts` → `lib/services/sse-connection.ts` |
95: | Interface | Factory function with callbacks for domain-specific handling |
96: | Benefits | Centralized lifecycle management, single fix point for bugs |
97: | Line reduction | ~70+ lines per consumer |
98: 
99: **When to use:** Same infrastructure pattern (EventSource lifecycle, reconnection, timers) appears in 2+ stores.
100: 

(File has more lines. Use 'offset' parameter to read beyond line 100)
</content>
